summary: Frontend-only testing of Industries (index + 9 detail pages) and Booking System solution page. All 61 functional checks passed: page loads, all 9 industry-card-{slug} testids, card structure (image, icon, price-from, short, Learn more arrow), booking-system-feature card, industry-page-{slug} for all 9 slugs, industry-cta-primary/secondary/final-cta, industry-why-0..3 (4 items), industry-faq-0..2 (3 items), industry-feature-* (8 each verified for doctors), industry-not-found fallback, page-booking-system, booking-hero-cta, booking-final-cta, booking-why-0..3, booking-step-0..4, booking-faq-0..4, booking-feature-* count = 12, header nav-industries (desktop) navigates to /industries, mobile-nav-m-industries present in mobile menu, home industries-view-all CTA + clickable industry-0 cards, GR/EN toggle correctly switches content on industries index, industry detail (doctors), and booking-system page.

issues: Greek heading uses italic display font that can look slightly off-balance vs. the rest of the site, but acceptable per existing typography

critical_code_review_comments: IndustryPage.jsx imports useLang's t but never uses it (minor unused destructure)
All routes, data-testids, i18n usage, and clickable links are correctly wired; no MongoDB/_id concerns (frontend only).

Images use Unsplash URLs — relevant to each vertical (corporate building, stethoscope, scales, brain, ship containers, plane/beach, hotel, key/villa, sailboat, mountains for booking). Looks appropriate.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Industries (9 slugs) + /solutions/booking-system fully verified. Slugs: corporate, doctors, lawyers, psychologists, shipping, tourism, hotels, rentals, yachts. Each industry has 4 why, ~8 features, 3 faq. Booking system has 4 why, 12 features, 5 process steps, 5 faq. Header nav-industries lives between Services and Pricing in desktop and mobile (mobile-nav-m-industries). Lang toggle uses 'ΕΛ' / 'EN' role=button in header — to test changes, click EN explicitly (not lang_toggle.first which is the active one).
